patch.value: GH-505: nested extension failures suggest irrelevant missing flags instead of preserving tracker recovery

When an extension command internally invokes another pm command and the nested command fails with a typed error (observed: tracker_not_initialized from 'list-all --limit 10000' inside pm-todos import against an uninitialized --pm-path), the outer command_failed envelope invents an unrelated missing option from the NESTED invocation: missing=['--limit'] + suggested_retry appending '--limit <value>' to the user's todos-import command line. --limit is not an importer flag and cannot fix the failure; the correct recovery (init or fix --pm-path) was already present in the nested error and gets discarded. Agents follow structured suggested_retry verbatim — this sends them toward an invalid command instead of the storage fix. Expected: preserve the nested typed error code/recovery when wrapping extension failures; never infer top-level missing flags from a nested command's fields; omit missing/suggested_retry unless the option belongs to the user-invoked command's contract.

Wrapped extension failures preserve the nested typed error code and recovery verbatim; missing/suggested_retry only ever reference options in the user-invoked command's contract; repro (todos import against uninitialized --pm-path) yields tracker_not_initialized recovery, not --limit; regression test covers nested-failure wrapping; GH-505 closed with a comment linking the fix

patch.value: Outer extension recovery suppresses inferred retries when a nested structured failure owns recovery.
Nested tracker recovery remains actionable and unrelated flags are never suggested.
Nested failures retain their recovery path without bogus outer missing flags or suggested retries.
Implemented nested extension recovery preservation with regression coverage.
